author: @yougeo
text:
Robert send out a message to all your members and ask them if they would continue their monthly support if you made all videos free so you can do a bigger godfundme as a general "give knowledge away for free youtuber" which would probably give you more success for a gofundme.

I think most members people who support you would continue to.

author: @yougeo
text:
I just read your comment about you are about to go broke and trying to 
keep your house.
"about to go broke mate - I am basically concentrating on keeping the 
doors open and not losing my house"
 
Dude. you need to top do a gofundme immediately. You are an excellent 
science educator with a long history of battery reveals. People would 
appreciate that. I think you would get a lot of support. 

And I dont mean it has to be gofundme. or that you have to have some 
product to sell to people who would fund you. I just mean do a general 
fund raiser so you can continue to provide do it yourself and science 
enthusiasts they latest "kitchen techniques" to keep up with batteries 
and graphene which remain two very hot subject now. (Personally I would 
move all your "junkyard recycling" project to a different channel.)

When you do your gofundme I think you have to flat out say you are going
 to lose your house because you have been giving away this information 
for years. You have to be real and upfront and DO THE ASK. People have 
to know you are SERIOUSLY IN NEED.  You cant act like everything is fine
 and you have a million opportunities in the hopper because that is what
 people will think and they will give to the other guy . You need to 
tell people you REALLY NEED HELP. 

My dad when he needed a job would try to project such a professional and
 knowledgeable image that no one knew he needed a job! You kind of do 
the same thing. Get ride of the logo and just be you trying to help 
others and giving away great battery and graphene tech on the internet 
for free for years.

You are a great science educator and have had a lot of influence. you 
have been followed by many with your early openness about graphene 
making techniques and batteries etc. You are sort of the "science Guy" 
of batteries and graphene. that's your real best and unique place on the
 internet and I think you might be able to raise some money to "keep the
 doors open and the videos coming for free" kind of thing. 

My suggestion is get rid of the members only content first so all videos
 are available to everyone and then go do a full gofundme. people in 
gofundme like people who don’t have additional paywalls. that has to be 
your whole pitch " I educate people for free and give away the latest 
greatest battery technology ideas and how to make them" 

For example the best battery collector video is one of your best and would get a lot of views 
but people wont see it because its members only. 

 Personally I would not stop paying you a members fee if you made all 
videos available for everyone. as I have said in the past I would give 
you the $5 a month anyway.  In fact I like to archive your videos and 
having the members only videos has actually hurt my ability to do that.

I dont know how much you get from those member fees but it cant be a 
lot. you probably would make more in one gofundme type raise than months
 of members fees. 
other do some "king of random " type stuff where you hook some homemade 
batteries to things to make them go BOOM. etc. each week a different 
kind of homemade battery to make something go BOOM.

also why not upload that graphic I made for you for cups and tshirts 
with you and the blowtorch? That would sell a lot better than the random
 logo that you sell. people want fun stuff and personality which you 
have plenty of. If you have lostthe graphic I think I still haveit on 
canva and can resend it.

just found this. sound like indiegogo and experiment and donorschoose 
might be good. remember you can do more than one at at time it think.

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: It reminds me of a lozenge shaped lotus f1 car from yesteryear, I've no clue about doctor who but I imagine a rubber ball honking horn to be almost compulsory!:) ps the fish eye effect of the go pro lens  looks like extreme tow out! Lol I think a George peppard cigar might have some in stitches!!! Hahahalol

replies.author: @jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering if it's got four wheels it's going to draw intrest! The black paint really pulls it together and already just at this stage it appears  to be purposely functional!

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Looking awesome Rob. I was thinking that you would end up fitting the gearbox with an actual tooth gear straight off the electric motor. Also looking forward to seeing the Ah rating of the battery you end up using. Anyway really looking forward to seeing this project in the final phases, wondering if you will try getting it registered for road use, hahaha 🤣👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I have been thinking about that ever since you mentioned it mate

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering a tooth geared gearbox would avoid slippage from a pulley based gearbox and give better torque.

replies.author: @kevinchastain727
replies.text: using a worm gear drive saw for the power plant may also be a option as they have a universal motor and will work on AC or DC input and could be connected directly to the input on the transmission.

author: @Killianwsh
text: She's looking sharper by the day Rob! Nice work!   Oh @ 00:38 Are you brewing up a few pints on your own there in the shop?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - you know me mate - always keeping busy and we have to wet the head of this with something lol

replies.author: @Killianwsh
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Would love to watch a video set on your set up! :)  I used to brew Meads ,etc.  15 -60 gal at a time back in the day.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: Are you talking about the wood gas generator that is sitting first thing in garage? Or is there something I'm missing?

author: @Stuartrusty
text: Excited to see it come this far! I guess like you I am old enough to remember the real thing. I can remember Bessie being displayed for a number of years at one of our local Ford car dealers. I'd then worked out why (at the time I didn't know) because the chassis was a 1954 Ford Popular 103E. I'm excited to see your example up and running. Nice job so far.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate - and yes I remember the original too!!

author: @popuptoaster
